title: Bulk report - Week 37
summary:
Capesize
Positive sentiment was observed across both the Atlantic and Pacific basins throughout the week, with the exception of a brief pause on Thursday. The Capesize timecharter average closed at $25,457 on Friday, marking a gain of $1,944 week-on-week. Sentiment appeared positive in the North Atlantic but activity remained limited overall.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: Positive sentiment was observed across both the Atlantic and Pacific basins throughout the week, with the exception of a brief pause on Thursday. The Capesize timecharter average closed at $25,457 on Friday, marking a gain of $1,944 week-on-week.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: A week for the optimists in the Panamax sector with steady rises throughout the Atlantic and Asian markets, although we seemed to have reach a period of consolidation as the week ended. From the Atlantic basin, we saw decent levels of both grain and mineral demand versus a limited tonnage list, creating the perfect storm for owners, reports of mid $20,000s achieved for a trans-Atlantic trip via US Gulf delivery this side.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: A positional week overall as the Atlantic remained firm in pockets whilst The Asian arena certainly in the south saw demand retract and rates ease a little. In the Atlantic, the main push came from the US Gulf, a 60,000-dwt fixing delivery Mobile for trip to India at around $32,000 level.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: The market displayed a mixed performance this week, with some regions holding steady while others recorded stronger gains. In the Continent and Mediterranean, activity was limited, with only marginal improvements as rates edged slightly higher than previous levels.
